text: The hydroselective capture of carbon dioxide is a pressing issue. We show that the interior of the metal-organic framework IRMOF-74-III can be covalently functionalized with primary amine and that this species is highly effective for selective capture of CO2 at 65% relative humidity. The synthesis, structure determination, and gas adsorption properties of the functionalized species of IRMOF-74-III (namely, the CH3, NH2, CH2NHCOBz, CH2NMeBoc, CH2NH2, and CH2NHMe analogues) and CO2 capture characteristics are presented. The (13)C cross-polarization magic angle spinning NMR spectra reveal the occurrence of a chemical binding of CO2 with the CH2NH2 and CH2NHMe surface species forming the carbamic products. The CO2 isotherms and the associated dynamic breakthrough experiments evidence that the CH2NH2 species of IRMOF-74-III-CH2NH2 is especially efficient for the capture of CO2 (3.2 mmol of CO2 per gram at 800 Torr) and for the removal of CO2 from wet nitrogen with a high value of breakthrough time of 610 ± 10 s g(-1) and total stability of the whole IRMOF structure.
